name: Ammunition (Firearm)
entries: You can use a firearm with the Ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition is part of the attack. The ammunition listed in parentheses with the property is the type of ammunition the weapon uses.

name: Ammunition
entries: You can use a weapon that has the Ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from it. The type of ammunition required is specified with the weapon's range. Each attack expends one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). After a fight, you can spend 1 minute to recover half the ammunition (round down) you used in the fight; the rest is lost.

name: Ammunition
entries: You can use a weapon that has the Ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a One-Handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.

If you use a weapon that has the Ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an {@item Improvised Weapon|XPHB}. A {@item Sling|XPHB} must be loaded to deal any damage when used in this way.

name: Burst Fire
entries: A weapon with the Burst Fire property can make a normal single-target attack, or it can spray a 10-foot-cube area within normal range with shots. Each creature in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC equal to 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Dexterity modifier) or take the weapon's normal damage. This action uses ten pieces of ammunition.

name: Finesse
entries: When making an attack roll with a Finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

name: Firearm
entries: You don't add your ability modifier to the weapon's damage, unless otherwise stated. Firearm ammunition is destroyed upon use.

name: Heavy
entries: You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls with a Heavy weapon if it's a Melee weapon and your Strength score isn't at least 13 or if it's a Ranged weapon and your Dexterity score isn't at least 13.

name: Heavy
entries: Small creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls with Heavy weapons. A Heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small creature to use effectively.

name: Light
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn and attack with a Light weapon, you can make one extra attack as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} later on the same turn. That extra attack must be made with a different Light weapon, and you don't add your ability modifier to the extra attack's damage unless that modifier is negative. See also the {@feat Two-Weapon Fighting|XPHB} feat and the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property entry on the Weapons table.

name: Loading
entries: You can fire only one piece of ammunition from a Loading weapon when you use an action, a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, or a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.

name: Range
entries: A Range weapon has a range in parentheses after the {@itemProperty A|XPHB|Ammunition} or {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second is the weapon's long range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the long range.

name: Range
entries: A weapon that has the Range property has a range in parentheses after the {@itemProperty A|XPHB|Ammunition} or {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second is the weapon's long range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the long range.

name: Reach
entries: A Reach weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you make a melee attack with it, as well as when you take the {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} action with it.

name: Reload
entries: You can make a limited number of shots with a Reload weapon. You must then reload the weapon as an action or a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

name: Reload
entries: A limited number of shots can be made with a weapon that has the Reload property. A character must then reload it using an action or a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} (the character's choice). The number of shots a weapon can make before being reloaded is shown in parentheses after the property's name.

name: Special
entries: A weapon with the Special property has unusual rules governing its use, which are explained in the weapon's description.

name: Thrown
entries: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack, and if the weapon is a Melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for the attack and damage rolls that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a {@item Handaxe|XPHB}, you use your Strength, but if you throw a {@item Dagger|XPHB}, you can use your Strength or Dexterity since the Dagger has the {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse} property.

name: Two-Handed
entries: A Two-Handed weapon requires two hands when you attack with it. You must use the weapon two-handed during the attack to gain the benefit of the property.

name: Versatile
entries: A Versatile weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

name: Vestige of Divergence
entries: This item is a Vestige of Divergence, see Vestiges of Divergence for more information.
